mission.title: cert-manager: Failed to generate serving certificate
mission.description: Failed to generate serving certificate. Users encounter: "in the logs of cert-manager webhook pod:".

steps.title: Identify cert-manager troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cert-manager deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=cert-manager
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `in the logs of cert-manager webhook pod:`

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Describe the bug**:

After we updated cert-manager to `v1.15.0`, we started to see the following error in the logs of cert-manager webhook pod:
`"Failed to generate serving certificate, retrying..."

steps.title: Apply the fix for Failed to generate serving certificate
steps.description:
Adds unit tests and intruces a few minor changes to improve logging and make the test work as expected. Should make the logs a lot more insightful.

### Kind

### Release Note
```yaml
$ kc -n cert-manager logs cert-manager-webhook-7476bf949b-95hzh
W0627 11:55:43.185374       1 client_config.go:659] Neither --kubeconfig nor --master was specified.  Using the inClusterConfig.  This might not work.
I0627 11:55:43.186580       1 webhook.go:128] "using dynamic certificate generating using CA stored in Secret resource" logger="cert-manager.webhook.webhook" secret_namespace="cert-manager" secret_name="cert-manager-webhook-ca"
I0627 11:55:43.279927       1 server.go:152] "listening for insecure healthz connections" logger="cert-manager.webhook" address=6080
I0627 11:55:43.28018
```

steps.title: Confirm Failed to generate serving certificate is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `in the logs of cert-manager webhook pod:` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Adds unit tests and intruces a few minor changes to improve logging and make the test work as expected. Should make the logs a lot more insightful.

### Kind

### Release Note. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

Same issue here @yinxulai 
> matic signing started working correctly. This workaround successfully resolved the problem in my environment.

No, it is not just registration in my case... when it is a new deployment of Cert Manager it does not register the certificate and therefore does not initialize correctly, however if it is an upgrade and you already had the certificate, the startup occurs without problem.
@yinxulai Thanks for the log output. The logging of this was improved for cert-manager 1.16.x. And I only see one "error" in your log:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
